100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On March 13, 1926, the British government announced plans to send additional troops to India in response to escalating unrest and protests against colonial rule. In the United States, the stock market showed signs of volatility, raising concerns among investors about the economic outlook. Meanwhile, in Germany, political tensions intensified as the Weimar Republic grappled with ongoing economic challenges and the rise of extremist factions.
Updated: Friday, March 13, 2026 at 07:27:03 AM CDT
